BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Monthly finance-review back-of-napkin after Medicare premium notice hinted Part B might rise next year. Checking household budget cushion.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate. FERS pension + Social Security cover fixed costs with wide margin; surplus goes to Navy Federal savings and TSP is left untouched.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Medicare Part B + Medigap rises to $370/mo
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed household costs $2,202 + $50 premium bump = $2,252. Guaranteed income $4,800/mo more than covers it; break-even needs only the pension+SS income stream (1 source).
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at $4,800/mo guaranteed income.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - major home repair adds $200/mo amortized (roof or HVAC)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Home maintenance fund and money-market reserve absorb it; no debt required.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even sits far below guaranteed monthly income. At $4,800/mo pension+SS against $2,202 expenses there is ample headroom. Main watch item is medical inflation, but reserves cover several years of buffer.
ActionItems: Confirm 2027 Medicare Part B premium once CMS announces
Revisit after year-end to top up home maintenance fund for roof inspection
